title: This NEW Tool Made Me DELETE Every Indicator from My Chart!
author: SoheilPKO
summary_bullets: This new indicator takes your trading to the next level.
This is an advanced indicator built based on multiple linear regression.
The accuracy of this indicator is insane.
I will teach you a trading strategy based on this indicator that makes consistent profit.
All right, let's add the indicator.
Here we click on indicators and we search regression slope oscillator this one which is a new indicator from Big Beluga.
All right, this is the indicator and as you can see it is added here below the chart.
As I said, this indicator is built based on multiple linear regression.
In case if you don't know what linear regression is, regression is a statistical technique to predict a variable based on the value of one or more variables.
And because of its linear characteristic, it's a perfect model in financial markets for predicting the price and trends in the market.

extracted_rules: In case if you don't know what linear regression is, regression is a statistical technique to predict a variable based on the value of one or more variables.
As you can see, when the oscillator line is below the center line, it shows that the market is trending down.
And when the oscillator line goes above the center line, it shows that the market is in an uptrend.
You may ask if this center line crossovers can be a signal for reversal of the market.
When crosses below the center line, it can be a signal for starting of a downtrend.
And when crosses above the center line, it can be a signal for starting of an uptrend.
When the oscillator line crosses above the signal line, it's a bullish signal and it shows that we have increased upside momentum.
And when the oscillator line crosses below the signal line, it's a bearish signal and it shows that we have increased downside momentum.

When it is a reversal signal on the other side, when the oscillator line is below the center line, if we get a bullish crossover, which is when the oscillator line crosses above the signal line, it shows a white circle like this.
And when the oscillator line is above the center line, if we get a bearish signal, which is when the oscillator line crosses below the signal line, we get a circle like this.
But instead, the signals that are in the same direction with the current trend of the market, they have lower risk and they can be used in trend following strategies.

For example, when the oscillator line is below the center line, which tells us that the market is trending down, then the bearish crossovers, which is when the oscillator line crosses below the signal line, they can be used for entering a trade in the same direction with the current trend of the market.

Or similarly here when the oscillator line is above the center line which tells us that the market is trending up then the bullish crossovers which is when the oscillator line crosses above the signal line they can be used for entering a trade in the same direction with the current trend of the market.
If the prior uptrend is still strong, the price will continue advancing.
But if a reversal is happening in the market and now we have greater downside momentum in the market, this advancing of the price will stop.
When we have this situation, it tells us that probably a pullback is forming on the chart.
In the next step, we want to know when the pullback is ended.
So the reversal signal here is confirmed and in practice we could enter a short position at this point with the stop loss above the pullback and profit target at risk-to-reward ratio of two.
If the reversal is real and now we have greater upside momentum, this decline of the price will stop and then advancing of the price to higher levels.
You can see that it happened here when this candle was closed.
